_meta.description: Common Core State Standards mapping for Atlas topics
_meta.note: Each topic maps to one or more CCSS domains/clusters. Mastery of a topic implies progress toward the listed standards.

Ratios & Proportional Relationships.description: Understand ratio concepts and use ratio reasoning to solve problems

Intro to Expressions & Equations.description: Apply and extend previous understandings of arithmetic to algebraic expressions

Area, Surface Area & Volume.description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume

The Number System (Decimals & Fractions).description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to divide fractions

Statistical Variability.description: Develop understanding of statistical variability and summarize distributions

Geometry on the Coordinate Plane.description: Draw polygons on the coordinate plane and find distances between points

Proportional Relationships.description: Analyze proportional relationships and use them to solve problems

Operations with Rational Numbers.description: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations with fractions

Expressions & Equations.description: Use properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions and solve problems

Geometry (Angles, Area, Volume).description: Draw, construct, and describe geometrical figures and solve problems

Statistics & Probability.description: Use random sampling, draw inferences, and investigate chance processes

Scale Drawings.description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures

Linear Equations & Inequalities.description: Analyze and solve linear equations and pairs of simultaneous linear equations

Functions & Graphing.description: Define, evaluate, and compare functions; use functions to model relationships

Geometry & Transformations.description: Understand congruence and similarity using physical models, transparencies, or geometry software

Statistics & Probability.description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data

Exponents & Scientific Notation.description: Work with radicals and integer exponents; use scientific notation

Pythagorean Theorem & Distance.description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem; solve problems involving volume

Linear & Quadratic Functions.description: Interpret and build functions; analyze linear and quadratic models

Systems of Equations.description: Solve systems of equations; represent and solve graphically

Polynomials & Factoring.description: Perform arithmetic operations on polynomials; understand the relationship between zeros and factors

Radical & Rational Expressions.description: Rewrite rational expressions; extend properties of exponents to rational exponents

Exponential Functions.description: Construct and compare linear, quadratic, and exponential models

Intro to Statistics.description: Summarize, represent, and interpret data on a single count or measurement variable

Geometric Proofs & Reasoning.description: Prove geometric theorems about lines, angles, triangles, and parallelograms

Similarity & Congruence.description: Understand similarity and congruence in terms of transformations

Right Triangle Trigonometry.description: Define trigonometric ratios and solve problems involving right triangles

Circles & Arc Measures.description: Understand and apply theorems about circles; find arc lengths and areas

Coordinate Geometry.description: Use coordinates to prove simple geometric theorems algebraically

Surface Area & Volume (3D).description: Explain volume formulas and use them to solve problems; visualize 3D objects

Trigonometric Functions.description: Extend the domain of trigonometric functions using the unit circle

Polynomial & Rational Functions.description: Understand the relationship between zeros and factors of polynomials

Exponential & Logarithmic Functions.description: Build and interpret exponential and logarithmic functions

Sequences & Series.description: Build functions that model arithmetic and geometric sequences

Conic Sections.description: Translate between geometric descriptions and equations for conics

Limits & Intro to Calculus.description: Extend function analysis toward limits and rates of change (beyond CC scope — college prep)

Limits & Continuity.description: Formal analysis of limits, continuity, and function behavior (beyond CC — AP level)

Derivatives & Applications.description: Rate of change and derivative applications (beyond CC — AP level)

Integrals & Applications.description: Accumulation functions and area under curves (beyond CC — AP level)

Differential Equations (Intro).description: Modeling with differential equations (beyond CC — AP level)

Probability & Combinatorics.description: Understand independence and conditional probability; use rules of probability

Vectors & Matrices.description: Represent and model with vector quantities; perform operations on matrices

Reading Comprehension Strategies.description: Cite textual evidence; determine themes and central ideas; analyze development

Narrative Writing.description: Write narratives with real or imagined experiences using effective technique

Grammar Basics (Parts of Speech).description: Demonstrate command of conventions of standard English grammar and usage

Vocabulary Building.description: Determine or clarify meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases

Informational Text Analysis.description: Analyze structure, point of view, and argument in informational texts

Speaking & Listening.description: Engage in collaborative discussions; present claims and findings

Literary Analysis (Theme & Character).description: Cite evidence, determine theme, analyze character and point of view interactions

Argumentative Writing (Intro).description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence

Grammar (Clauses & Phrases).description: Demonstrate command of conventions; use phrases and clauses correctly

Vocabulary & Word Roots.description: Use Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to meaning

Poetry Analysis.description: Analyze the impact of rhyme, structure, and form on meaning in poetry

Research Skills (Intro).description: Conduct short research projects; gather relevant information from multiple sources

Reading Comprehension & Analysis.description: Cite strongest textual evidence; determine themes; analyze connections

Essay Structure & Argumentation.description: Write arguments to support claims with logical reasoning and relevant evidence

Grammar & Sentence Structure.description: Use verbs in active/passive voice, conditional/subjunctive mood; form and use punctuation

Vocabulary & Context Clues.description: Use context, affixes, roots, and reference materials to determine word meanings

Literary Devices & Figurative Language.description: Analyze how differences in points of view create effects; interpret figurative language

Research & Citation Skills.description: Conduct research projects; gather and assess credibility of sources; cite sources

Short Story & Novel Analysis.description: Cite strong evidence; determine themes; analyze complex characters and structure

Rhetorical Analysis.description: Analyze how authors use rhetoric, structure, and point of view

MLA Research Writing.description: Write arguments and informative texts; conduct research with MLA format

Advanced Grammar & Style.description: Use parallel structure, various phrase types; apply conventions with sophistication

Shakespeare (Intro).description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning structure and language create meaning

Persuasive Speaking.description: Initiate and participate in discussions; present information clearly and persuasively

World Literature & Cultural Context.description: Analyze how cultural context shapes themes and perspectives in world literature

Comparative Literary Analysis.description: Analyze representations of a subject in two different mediums or texts

Synthesis Essay Writing.description: Write arguments and informative texts synthesizing multiple sources

Vocabulary in Context (SAT Prep).description: Interpret words in context; analyze nuances of word meaning; acquire vocabulary

Drama & Poetry Analysis.description: Analyze cumulative impact of word choices; analyze structure of drama and poetry

Media Literacy & Rhetoric.description: Evaluate argument and rhetoric in various media; assess credibility of sources

American Literature (Survey).description: Analyze foundational U.S. documents and seminal American literary works

AP-Style Rhetorical Analysis.description: Analyze effectiveness of structure and rhetoric in seminal texts

Argumentative & Research Essays.description: Write arguments and research papers with sustained complexity

Advanced Vocabulary & Diction.description: Determine or clarify meaning of unknown words; demonstrate understanding of figurative language

Satire & Social Commentary.description: Analyze impact of author's choices regarding satire, irony, and tone

College Application Writing.description: Write narratives; produce clear, coherent writing appropriate to task and audience

British & World Literature.description: Analyze multiple interpretations; demonstrate knowledge of foundational works

Literary Criticism & Theory.description: Apply critical lenses; analyze author's purpose and perspective

Senior Research Capstone.description: Conduct sustained research projects; synthesize multiple sources

Advanced Rhetoric & Style.description: Apply understanding of syntax and rhetoric for effective communication

Contemporary Issues in Literature.description: Analyze multiple sources; integrate and evaluate information from diverse formats

Professional & Technical Writing.description: Produce clear, coherent technical and professional writing
